text.title: Creating or Updating Claude Code Agents Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent spawned to run a single `grep` call. 12K tokens of context for a 50-token task.
antipattern.root-cause: Author assumed parallelism win; the subtask completed in one tool call where the parent could have run directly.
antipattern.fix: Run inline in parent for < 3-tool-call tasks. Reserve agents for > 5K-token subtasks.

antipattern.symptom: Parent passes a dict by reference; subagent mutates it; parent sees no change because process boundary.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated Task tool as a function call.
antipattern.fix: Pass paths to files; subagent writes a result file the parent reads after Task returns.

antipattern.symptom: Agent returns 3 paragraphs of natural language. Parent has to LLM-parse to extract decisions.
antipattern.fix: Declare output_contract.kind = json + schema_uri. Agent prompt enforces the shape.

antipattern.symptom: Agent gets full tool whitelist 'just in case'. Bug causes Bash to wipe a dir.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to broad permissions to avoid wiring per-agent whitelists.
antipattern.fix: Whitelist minimum tools. Read-only research agent should have only Read+Grep+Glob.
